task_name: Laparoscopic Urological Surgery Course Promotional/Educational Video
reasoning_for_classification: This video is a promotional and educational compilation for a 'Laparoscopic Urological Surgery Course for Residents' directed by Jungle Chi-Hsiang WU and Yao-Chou TSAI. It contains course overview, instructor introductions, teaching vignettes about laparoscopic fundamentals, equipment demonstrations, and calls-to-action for registration. There is no single FLS task being performed by a trainee; instead, multiple educational segments are presented with narration, graphics overlays, and instructor commentary.
video_content_summary: Promotional course video featuring: (1) title slides identifying the course as a Laparoscopic Urological Surgery Course for Residents; (2) instructor interviews and facility tours; (3) educational content on laparoscopic equipment, tools, energy sources, and pneumoperitoneum; (4) brief demonstrations of instrument handling and knot-tying techniques by instructors; (5) course highlights and objectives; (6) call-to-action messaging ('Register now', 'Guarantee your vacancy'); (7) closing credits with course directors' names.

demonstrated_techniques: Basic laparoscopic instrument handling and grasping
Proper positioning and use of needle drivers
Hand coordination and instrument exchange during suturing
verbal_cues: To prepare resident doctors ready for safe practice of laparoscopic surgery
To describe and understand the equipment, tools, energy, and pneumoperitoneum
To present the fundamentals of laparoscopic techniques
Course is designed to prepare resident doctors for safe laparoscopic surgical practice
common_errors_addressed: Proper instrument grip and positioning (implicitly demonstrated through correct technique)
Systematic approach to knot-tying with alternating hand control
Understanding equipment and pneumoperitoneum management before hands-on practice
teaching_content.target_audience: Resident physicians preparing for laparoscopic surgical practice
course_objectives_identified: Prepare residents for safe laparoscopic surgery practice
Provide hands-on training with simulated models
Build competency in instrument handling and knot-tying

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional/promotional is definitive based on presence of: (1) course title slides and branding, (2) instructor introductions and facility tours, (3) multiple narrator overlays and educational graphics, (4) no single continuous FLS task performance, (5) registration calls-to-action. This is clearly an educational/marketing video, not a scored performance evaluation.
cannot_determine: Specific FLS task performance metrics — no single task is performed to completion
Trainee performance score — video is not a performance evaluation
Individual task timing — demonstrations are brief and integrated into instructional narrative
recommendation: This video should NOT be scored using FLS rubrics. It is an institutional/course promotional video with educational content. Any individual technique demonstrations within the video are fragmentary and embedded in instructional context, not formal task performances. Instructors shown are demonstrating correct technique for teaching purposes, not undergoing timed evaluation.
